old_claim.note: Technical Drawing Standards provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No drawing-standard dependency remains because the 1711 anchor is the first detailed written specification, not the later distinct practice of patent drawings.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract identifies the 1711 innovation as a specification containing a detailed description and does not make drawings part of that first record.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The first detailed British patent disclosure was a descriptive specification dating to 1711.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The repository already has a separate 1790 patent-drawing node, so importing that later format would collapse distinct evidence.

ontology_before: The first detailed specification was bundled with later patent-drawing conventions.
ontology_after: The 1711 written-disclosure node remains distinct from the 1790 patent specification drawings node.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: printed_patent_specifications no longer directly depends on technical_drawing_standards.
would_reject_if: The 1711 source were shown to require standardized technical drawings as part of the first detailed specification.
short_reason: The first detailed specification was not scoped as a drawing system.
